calDavSettings.title: Calendar (CalDAV)

notifications.smartNotificationsDesc: Only notify for selected categories and VIP senders
notifications.vipSendersDesc: These senders always trigger notifications regardless of category
notifications.osPermissionDeniedBody: Notifications are enabled in Melo, but the OS is blocking them. Allow Melo in System Settings → Notifications.

composing.sendAndArchiveDesc: Automatically archive threads after sending a reply

accounts.resyncConfirmMessage: This deletes ALL locally stored messages, threads and sync state for this account, then re-downloads them from the server. Local-only data (offline drafts, task ↔ email links) may not survive. Only messages still present on the server within your sync window will be restored. Continue?
accounts.defaultAccountDesc: The default account is used when no account is selected — for example composing a new email from the unified inbox.
accounts.aliasesDesc: Aliases let you send email from different addresses using the same account.
accounts.noAliases: No send-as aliases configured for this account.
accounts.fullResyncDesc: Re-downloads all emails. Use if your inbox appears out of sync.
accounts.syncChangesNote: Changes apply to the next full resync.
accounts.unfetchableRetriesDesc: How many times to retry a message the server refuses to serve before skipping it (it stays counted and shown as a sync warning, and is retried again if the server later provides it).
accounts.pendingOpsDesc: Operations queued while offline, waiting to be sent.
accounts.failedOpsDesc: Operations that could not be completed after multiple retries.

calendar.noCalendarsSynced: No calendars synced yet. Open the calendar view to trigger a sync.
calendar.calendarComingSoon: Support for standalone calendar accounts (e.g. iCloud, Nextcloud) is coming soon.
calendar.invitePruningDesc: Calendar invites older than this period are automatically archived and removed from your inbox on launch.

shortcuts.quickComposeDesc: Open compose window from any app
shortcuts.rebindHint: Click a shortcut to rebind it. Press any key or key combination to set.

intelligence.onDeviceTitle: 100% On-device — no data leaves your machine
intelligence.onDeviceDesc: Melo uses a local Ollama server ({url}) to generate vector embeddings of your emails. These embeddings are stored in the local SQLite database and never sent to any external server. Semantic search uses cosine similarity computed in-process.
intelligence.enableSemanticDesc: Index emails locally and mix vector similarity into Ask My Inbox results (40% keyword + 60% semantic)
intelligence.semanticRagDesc: When enabled, Ask My Inbox combines keyword search (FTS5) with vector similarity for smarter, context-aware results. Emails are indexed in the background in small batches — the app stays responsive.
intelligence.indexedAccountsDesc: Enable semantic indexing per account. Only enabled accounts are indexed by the background backfill job and included in Ask My Inbox semantic results.
intelligence.embeddingModelDesc: Configure the Ollama server and model used for generating email embeddings. The server URL is shared with the AI tab.
intelligence.modelNotFound: Model not found — run: ollama pull {model}
intelligence.testFailed: Test failed — check server URL and model name
intelligence.chunkSizeDesc: How much text to embed per email. Larger values capture more context but increase Ollama memory usage. Default 512 ≈ 2 KB of text; max for nomic-embed-text is 8192 tokens.
intelligence.batchSizeDesc: Emails processed per indexing cycle (50ms pause between each). Higher values speed up indexing but increase CPU load — keep at 5–20 for background comfort.
intelligence.enableForAccount: Enable semantic indexing for at least one account above to start indexing.
intelligence.allIndexed: All emails indexed. Semantic search is fully operational.
intelligence.indexingComplete: Indexing complete — {count} emails had no embeddable content
intelligence.reindexConfirm: This will delete all {count} embeddings and re-index from scratch. Continue?
intelligence.eligibilityNote: * Eligible messages exclude Spam, Trash, and accounts where semantic search is disabled. Sentinels mark messages that were processed but had insufficient text for embedding.
intelligence.temporalAgingDesc: Urgency naturally fades as threads grow older. Between the start and floor day, the score linearly decays toward a dim minimum. Beyond the floor, urgency is silenced automatically.
intelligence.decayStartDesc: Urgency is unchanged until this many days after the last message.
intelligence.decayFloorDesc: At this age, urgency reaches its minimum (dim indicator, not hidden). Must be > Decay Start.
intelligence.decayExample: Example: with Start=20 and Floor=30, a thread that's 25 days old loses 50% of its urgency. At 30+ days it shows only a faint indicator.
intelligence.priorityDomainsDesc: Emails from these domains, or emails mentioning new projects and quotes, receive a +0.15–0.3 urgency boost regardless of keywords. Comma-separated.
intelligence.autoLabelDesc: Automatically assign your custom labels to incoming emails. The AI analyzes subject, sender, and body in cascade — it only assigns a label when confidence meets the threshold.
intelligence.enableAutoLabelDesc: Incoming emails are analyzed and labeled automatically when the AI confidence score meets the minimum threshold. Runs on new mail only — not during re-sync.
intelligence.autoLabelThresholdDesc: Only assign a label if the AI confidence score reaches this value (50–95%). Higher values reduce false assignments. Default: 75%.
intelligence.autoLabelAccountsDesc: Enable auto-labeling per account. Only enabled accounts have their incoming emails auto-labeled.

tasks.retentionDesc: Control how long deleted and completed tasks are kept before being permanently removed.
tasks.deletedRetentionDesc: Days before manually deleted tasks are purged from the database
tasks.autoHideCompletedDesc: Hours after which completed tasks disappear from the active view (0 = hide immediately)
tasks.completedRetentionDesc: Days before completed tasks are permanently deleted (0 = keep forever)
tasks.trashRecoveryDesc: Deleted tasks are soft-deleted and visible in the Trash view inside the Tasks page. You can restore or permanently delete them from there.
tasks.trashRecoveryDesc2: When viewing a thread, a Restore banner appears in the task sidebar if there are recoverable tasks for that thread.

about.openDevToolsDesc: Open the WebView developer tools inspector
about.appDescription: A fast, open-source desktop email client built with privacy in mind. Your emails stay on your machine — no cloud, no tracking.
about.licenseText: Licensed under the GNU General Public License, version 3 or later. You may obtain a copy of the License at
about.licenseText2: Copyright 2026 Melo contributors. You may use, distribute, and modify this software under the terms of the GPLv3 (or later). Melo is a fork of Velo (Apache 2.0); portions remain under that license. This software is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND.

database.autoMaintenanceDesc: Melo automatically refreshes query planner statistics (PRAGMA optimize) once a day in the background. This keeps search and inbox queries fast without any user action.
database.compactDesc: Deleting and moving emails leaves unused space inside the database file. Compacting rebuilds the file to reclaim that space. This can take a while on large mailboxes and needs free disk space roughly equal to the current database size — it won't delete any of your data.
database.compactConfirmMessage: This rebuilds the database file to reclaim unused space. It may take a while and temporarily needs extra free disk space. Your emails will not be affected.

general.languageRestartNote: Restart the app to apply the new language.
general.launchAtLoginDesc: Start Melo automatically when you log in (minimized to tray)
general.imapPushDesc: Use a persistent connection per IMAP folder so new mail arrives in seconds instead of waiting for the next poll. Falls back to polling when the server does not support IDLE.
general.blockRemoteImagesDesc: Hides tracking pixels and remote images until you choose to load them
general.phishingDetectionDesc: Scan message links for phishing indicators and show warnings

mailRules.labelsDesc: Create, rename, recolor, delete, or reorder your labels.
mailRules.filtersDesc: Filters automatically apply actions to new incoming emails during sync.
mailRules.smartLabelsDesc: Describe what emails should get a label using plain English. AI automatically labels matching emails during sync.
mailRules.smartFoldersDesc: Smart folders are saved searches that automatically show matching emails.
mailRules.quickStepsDesc: Quick steps let you chain multiple actions together into a single click. Apply them from the right-click menu on any thread.
mailRules.imapFoldersDesc: Manage your IMAP mailbox folders — create, rename, or delete custom folders.

people.contactsDesc: Contacts are automatically added when you send or receive emails. Edit display names or remove contacts below.
people.subscriptionsDesc: View all detected newsletter and promotional senders. Unsubscribe using RFC 8058 one-click POST, mailto, or browser fallback.

ai.providerDesc: Choose which AI provider to use for summarization, compose assistance, and smart categorization.
ai.ollamaNoKey: Connect to a local Ollama or LMStudio server. No API key required.
ai.enableAIDesc: Master toggle for all AI functionality
ai.autoCategorizeDesc: Use AI to refine rule-based categorization
ai.autoSummarizeDesc: Show AI summaries on multi-message threads
ai.phishingAiVerdictDesc: Let the AI re-check flagged emails to dismiss false positives (e.g. newsletters), so warnings appear only for real threats
ai.autoDraftDesc: Pre-populate the reply editor with an AI-generated draft
ai.learnWritingStyleDesc: Analyze your sent emails to match your tone and voice
ai.reanalyzeDesc: Reanalyze your writing style from recent sent emails
ai.soulDesc: Define the AI's personality and behavior. Edit SOUL.md to customize how the assistant communicates.
ai.bundlingDesc: Collapse categories into a single row in the inbox. Optionally set a delivery schedule to batch emails.
ai.behavioralIntelligenceDesc: Controls whether Melo tracks sender patterns, urgency signals, and reputation to adapt inbox prioritization to your behavior.
ai.enableBehavioralDesc: Master switch — disabling this turns off urgency scoring, reputation tracking, and the Heat Extinguisher
ai.urgencyIndicatorsDesc: Show Zap and Heat icons on threads based on urgency score — turn off if you prefer a quieter inbox
ai.senderReputationDesc: Melo tracks when you silence urgency from a sender. Senders muted repeatedly within the forgiveness window receive a lower urgency weight automatically.
ai.forgivenessWindowDesc: How far back to look when counting mutes. After this period, the sender's score resets.
ai.muteThresholdDesc: Number of mutes before a 50% urgency penalty is applied. Higher = more tolerant.
ai.smartAutoExtinguishDesc: When you reply to an urgent thread, Melo uses AI to evaluate if the reply resolves the concern — and clears the urgency indicator only if it does
ai.categoriesDesc: Incoming emails are automatically sorted using rule-based heuristics (Gmail labels, sender domain, headers). When AI is enabled, it refines results for better accuracy.
ai.categoriesAutoArchiveDesc: Enable auto-archive to skip the inbox for specific categories.
ai.copilotDesc: Uses {model}. Requires a GitHub PAT with models:read permission.

sounds.volumeDesc: Master volume for all sound events
sounds.eventsDesc: Choose which events trigger a sound. Click the play button to preview.

events.sendDesc: A short click when your email is successfully dispatched
events.receiveDesc: A gentle two-tone when a new email arrives in your inbox
events.taskCompleteDesc: A crisp metallic note when you mark a task as done
events.eventAlertDesc: Two mechanical pulses when a calendar event is approaching
events.sendErrorDesc: A low thud when an email could not be sent or was queued due to an error
events.shortcutClickDesc: A barely-audible micro-click when using keyboard shortcuts (disabled by default)

imapFolderEditor.deleteMessage: Delete folder "{{name}}"? This action cannot be undone.
imapFolderEditor.noLabels: No user labels available for this account.

trashToolbar.emptyTrashConfirmMessage: All trashed messages will be permanently deleted. Conversations made up only of trashed messages will be removed; conversations with other messages will keep them.

spamToolbar.trashAllConfirmMessage: All spam conversations will be moved to the trash.

phishing.linksDetectedPlural: {count} suspicious links found in this message. Be cautious before clicking any links.

authWarning.spfFail: SPF authentication failed — this email may be spoofed
authWarning.dkimFail: DKIM signature invalid — this email may have been tampered with

imapIdle.idleFoldersDesc: Folders that use push (IDLE) for instant notification. Others use 60s polling.
imapIdle.globalDisabled: IMAP push is disabled globally (Settings → General → IMAP push)
imapIdle.noFoldersSync: No IMAP folders found — sync the account first.
imapIdle.selectedDesc: Selected folders receive new mail in seconds via a persistent connection. Unselected folders are checked every 60 s.

unfetchableMessages.title: Messages not downloaded
unfetchableMessages.description: The mail server lists these messages but repeatedly failed to deliver them, so they are missing locally. Melo will retry them if the server serves them again. Opening the mailbox in your provider's webmail and moving or re-saving the message there often fixes the problem.
unfetchableMessages.settingsDesc: Messages the server refused to deliver after several attempts. Ignoring an entry removes it from the sidebar sync warning; you can restore it here at any time.
unfetchableMessages.settingsHint: You can also manage these messages later from Settings → Accounts → Skipped messages.
unfetchableMessages.empty: No skipped messages — everything the server lists has been downloaded.
unfetchableMessages.contextUnknown: No neighboring messages found locally to estimate its position

outgoing.sendFailedBody: Could not send email. It's kept in Outgoing — check your SMTP settings.
outgoing.sendFailedBodyWithError: Could not send email: {error}. It's kept in Outgoing.
outgoing.queuedBody: Could not reach the server. The email is kept in Outgoing and will be retried automatically — you'll be notified if it still can't be sent.
outgoing.interruptedBody: The app closed during a send. The email is kept in Outgoing — check your Sent folder, then retry it.
outgoing.opFailedBody: A pending "{operation}" operation failed permanently. Check Outgoing in the sidebar.
outgoing.scheduledFailedBody: "{subject}" could not be sent. It's kept in Scheduled — retry it from there.

notifications.syncCatchUp: {count} new emails arrived while sync was catching up

account.reauthBannerDesc: Google rejected the saved credentials for {email}. Mail sync is paused until you sign in again.

errors.migrationFailedBody: Melo could not update its local database to the latest version. To protect your data, the app won't start until this is resolved. Retrying often fixes transient problems.
errors.migrationBackupHint: Your emails on the server are safe. If the problem persists, back up the melo.db file in the app data folder before reinstalling.
